# Pixel Puppets

> Live interactive theatre where performers drive digital characters in real time on a cinema-scale screen, with the audience shaping what happens next. Theatre meets cinema meets gaming. In development since 2024.

## What we do

Pixel Puppets is a new format of interactive theatre. Costumed performers appear in front of a cinema-scale screen, using game controllers, live voice and facial tracking to bring digital characters to life as the audience watches and shapes the show.

The work sits at the intersection of theatre, cinema and live gaming. It's designed for auditorium audiences without headsets or VR equipment — what's sometimes called "extended reality theatre" or "XR theatre" presented as live cinema rather than VR.

## Where we're based

A Yorkshire-based theatre company working across:
- Sheffield, South Yorkshire, United Kingdom
- Holmfirth, West Yorkshire, United Kingdom

## Project status

In funded research and development. Currently building a 20-minute vertical slice as proof of concept.

Building piece by piece:
- Format: 20-minute vertical slice
- Story: scripted comedy meets live improv meets audience chaos
- Puppetry: game controllers, live voice, facial capture
- Screen: cinema-scale projection, performers on stage in front
- Latency: getting the screen to react instantly to the performer

## Team

- Ben Carlin — Artistic Director
- Joe Duggan — Producer
- Daniel Bacchus — Lead Development
- Various Artists Studio — Characters and Animation

## Funding and support

- BFI Immersive Fiction Lab (early concept development, initial R&D)
- Immersive Arts UK (Experiment Strand, current R&D phase)
- Arts Council England (R&D Grant, current R&D phase)

## Looking for

Festival programmers, theatre venues, immersive performance curators and commissioners interested in new live formats. Especially programmers exploring:
- Interactive theatre
- Immersive theatre
- Digital theatre
- Extended reality theatre / XR theatre
- Theatre meets cinema
- Theatre meets gaming
- Audience-driven performance
- New theatre formats
